top_categories.workout_context: Gran Fondo Asheville's mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 100 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Asheville's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
80ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At mile 60 of 100, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
8,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At Gran Fondo Asheville, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Asheville. Train it until it feels boring.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Asheville, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 100 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 8,000ft of climbing on mountain passes does to your power file on race day.
8,000ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Asheville’s 100 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Gran Fondo Asheville. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at Gran Fondo Asheville demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride mountain passes at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 100 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 100 miles of mountain passes in Asheville Yards Amphitheater, 75 Coxe Ave, Asheville, NC 28801.
